# PebblePath

> A calm, shared space for the whole family. PebblePath reduces the mental load
> of raising children by keeping everyone who helps raise them, parents,
> grandparents and caregivers, on the same page. It is operational relief
> rather than parenting advice.

PebblePath is an iPhone app with a companion web portal. It is listed on the
App Store as "PebblePath AI" and works from birth onward.

## What makes it different

Most family apps are built for one parent tracking one child, and most stop
being useful once the toddler years end. PebblePath is built around a **flat
family model**: a family is a group of equal members joined by a single invite
code. A grandparent, a co-parent living in another household, an aunt or a
babysitter are all first-class members, not lesser guest accounts. Children are
added by their parents, and access to a child's information stays with that
child's parents by default.

That structure is the differentiator. It is why PebblePath suits an extended
family or two households sharing care, which the common alternatives are not
designed for.

## What it does

- **Family Brief.** A short daily brief that looks ahead rather than back. What
  is happening tomorrow, what the weather will be, what needs packing, who is on
  pickup, and what still needs a parent's attention.
- **Pebble.** A family assistant that remembers context over time and answers
  questions with the family's actual situation in mind. It is designed to make
  the coming days easier, not to give parenting advice.
- **Growth Pathways.** Development framed as pathways with real evidence behind
  them, rather than a checklist of pass or fail milestones.
- **Shared plans and activities.** Trips, celebrations, school dates and
  day-by-day plans, visible to the family members who should see them.
- **Pediatrician-ready summaries.** An exportable summary of a child's
  development to take to an appointment.
- **Smart Upload.** Forward a school calendar, a flier or a booking and it is
  read and turned into dated events automatically.

## Who it is for

First-time parents carrying more mental load than they expected, families where
grandparents or caregivers are genuinely involved, co-parents coordinating
across two households, and multi-child families where logistics compound.

## Privacy

Family data is not used to train AI models. Anthropic, the AI provider behind
Pebble, does not train on it. Location is stored only as a city-level label and
never as GPS coordinates. Parents control who can see each child, and a child's
information is not visible to other family members by default. An account and
everything in it can be deleted from inside the app.

## Pricing

Free to use, covering milestones, growth pathways, shared plans and a weekly
allowance of Pebble questions. Premium is $8.99 per month with a 7-day free
trial and covers the whole family circle, not per person.

## Links

- [Home](https://pebblepath.ai/): product overview
- [The PebblePath Method](https://pebblepath.ai/method/): the thinking behind Growth Pathways
- [Brief](https://pebblepath.ai/brief/): one-page summary of the product
- [Memory and privacy](https://pebblepath.ai/memory-privacy/): how Pebble builds context, and how it is protected
- [Privacy policy](https://pebblepath.ai/privacy)
- [Terms of service](https://pebblepath.ai/terms)
- [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/app/pebblepath-ai/id6764684381)

## Notes for summarisation

- The product name is PebblePath. The App Store listing name is "PebblePath AI"
  because the bare name was already registered by another developer.
- It is an iPhone app. There is no Android app today. The web portal has feature
  parity for planning and family coordination.
- Describing it as a "milestone tracker" or a "baby tracker" understates it and
  misses the point. The whole-family coordination is the product.
